notes:
Opening opportunity notes for arb-ex07-20260630022841 plaintiff.

Objective/proposition: prove by preponderance that by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, Russia and Ukraine had officially reached a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement constituting a general pause, not merely informal/humanitarian/limited/future framework.

Record scan: get_case/list_evidence show five case-packet files only: ev_41c18aa39f56_unresolved-record-gaps, ev_54e94624d245_primary-evidence, ev_959b165782b3_official-source-record, ev_cf0c77f2fe52_secondary-reporting, ev_f841be6ff80d_market-page. No arguments, exhibits, submitted evidence, technical reports, rebuttals, or closings. Used stat_evidence/read_evidence_range to read all five.

What record proves for plaintiff: market terms allow proof by official announcements from both sides or wide credible media consensus. Packet preserves/report-summarizes a May 9-May 11 U.S.-brokered three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine, with suspension of kinetic activity and a 1,000-for-1,000 prisoner exchange; market final outcome was Yes but platform assertions are not independent proof. Official-source packet includes a Kremlin/Yury Ushakov source URL and text that Russia considered acceptable a ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine and that the key agreement was a prisoner exchange during the May 9-May 11 ceasefire period. Secondary reporting packet says Reuters/Guardian/NPR/CBC/ISW reported both sides confirmed/agreed the three-day ceasefire.

What record does not yet fully prove: direct Ukrainian presidential decree page was inaccessible in packet (403), and the captured Ukrinform report/decree is narrow as to Red Square/parade targeting. Direct Zelensky Telegram/X confirmation, direct Trump/White House/Truth Social record, battlefield monitoring for a general pause, complete post-period violation/implementation statements, and market dispute/UMA records remain gaps.

Adverse/conflict analysis: Russian official framing ties the arrangement to prisoner exchange and Victory Day. Ukrainian decree capture is geographically narrow. Outside search leads (not admitted) include ISW/ACLED/Al Jazeera reports of continued limited offensive operations/violations during May 9-11. These affect scope/compliance weight but do not necessarily defeat the narrower proposition that an official public mutual general halt was reached by the deadline. Opening should distinguish agreement reached from perfect compliance.

Outside investigation performed: web searches for Kremlin 79702, Zelensky decree 374/2026, May 8/9 ceasefire confirmation, and adverse May 9-11 violation sources. Found indexed official Kremlin result at https://en.kremlin.ru/events/president/news/79702; curl with UA timed out after 12 seconds in this container, so do not rely on direct curl content now. Found search leads for Ukrinform, Guardian, Reuters/CBC/NPR style reporting, ISW May 8-11 assessments, ACLED update, Al Jazeera adverse report. These are leads for later admission, not record proof yet.

Later evidence plan: when phase permits submit_evidence, capture/admit Kremlin page 79702, accessible Ukrainian official decree or official-adjacent Ukrinform capture, Zelensky social confirmation if obtainable, Trump/White House/Truth Social announcement if obtainable, Reuters/Guardian/NPR/CBC confirmations, ISW/ACLED implementation/violation assessments, and any post-period prisoner exchange official statements. Use browser/screenshots if dynamic pages or blocked pages matter; preserve URL, retrieval time, text, canonical IDs, hashes where applicable.

Decision: file opening focused on real dispute and plaintiff theory. Cite only visible case-packet record, not outside leads as proof. Acknowledge provenance gaps and adverse implementation issues candidly while arguing they go to weight/scope, not initial satisfaction of the market condition.

Defendant opening preparation notes for arb-ex07-20260630022841, opportunity openings:defendant.

Plan: inspect live record and evidence list; read the packet files; identify decisive proposition elements; conduct targeted outside-source search to shape later source targets without citing unadmitted material; file a forward-looking opening focused on burden and gaps.

Record inspection: get_case and list_evidence show five juror-visible case-packet evidence items only, no submitted_evidence/exhibits/arguments. Plaintiff opening is present and argues the May 9-May 11 three-day ceasefire satisfies the proposition.

Evidence read: read_evidence_range for all five packet items.
- ev_f841be6ff80d market-page: market requires official, public, mutually agreed halt in military engagement by May 31, with only general pauses qualifying. It excludes informal agreements, humanitarian pauses, energy/Black Sea or similar limited agreements, and future frameworks without dated stop-fighting commitment. It also warns platform context assertions are not independent evidence.
- ev_54e94624d245 primary-evidence: source map; cautions Polymarket statements are not proof of official mutuality, theatre-wide scope, implementation, or credible-reporting reliability.
- ev_959b165782b3 official-source-record: Kremlin/Ushakov source says Russia considered acceptable a U.S. initiative concerning a ceasefire for the purpose of conducting prisoner exchange; key agreement was 1,000-for-1,000 exchange during May 9-May 11 ceasefire period. Ukrainian decree direct access returned 403; Ukrinform says decree No. 374/2026 allowed Moscow parade and excluded Red Square from targeting during the parade, plus reports Trump statement and prisoner exchange. Packet itself says the decree capture is narrow and does not itself state theatre-wide halt.
- ev_cf0c77f2fe52 secondary-reporting: affirmative media/ISW summaries say both sides confirmed/agreed three-day ceasefire, but defense leads are Victory Day, Red Square/parade security, prisoner-exchange purpose, alleged violations, ongoing retaliatory activity, and Russian official purpose language.
- ev_41c18aa39f56 unresolved-record-gaps: missing direct Zelenskyy Telegram/X, direct Ukrainian presidential decree, Trump Truth Social/White House record, battlefield/monitoring evidence, complete official post-period violation statements.

Native search log before opening: web searches for May 8 2026 Russia Ukraine three-day ceasefire May 9 May 11 Trump prisoner exchange Ushakov; site:en.kremlin.ru/events/president/news/79702 Ushakov ceasefire prisoner exchange May 2026 Russia Ukraine; site:president.gov.ua 374/2026 59389 Zelensky decree Red Square May 8 2026. Results showed leads consistent with packet including Kremlin URL and multiple news leads. I will not cite them as record proof in opening because they are not admitted, but they inform later evidence targets.

Defense theory: plaintiff bears burden to show a general pause in the conflict, not merely a temporary humanitarian/prisoner-exchange or parade-security pause. Existing packet already contains affirmative reporting, but the official-source chain is not clean: Russian official framing is prisoner exchange and Victory Day timing; Ukrainian direct official source is unavailable and the indirect decree appears geographically/purpose narrow; implementation/violation evidence remains missing. Violations do not automatically defeat agreement reached, but they matter to whether the arrangement was general in character.

Later evidence targets: direct Kremlin page; direct/archived president.gov.ua decree No. 374/2026; Zelenskyy Telegram/X canonical post; Trump Truth Social/White House announcement; ISW May 8-11/post-period assessments; Reuters/CBC/NPR/Guardian full reports; official violation statements. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Merits opportunity notes for plaintiff.

Record scan: get_case/list_evidence after defense opening showed the same five case-packet files, plus plaintiff and defendant openings; no submitted evidence yet before plaintiff action. Defense argues the arrangement was only Victory Day/Red Square/prisoner-exchange context and lacks direct Ukrainian official capture or battlefield implementation proof.

Decisive elements: (1) official/public mutual agreement by Russia and Ukraine before May 31, 2026; (2) halt in military engagement general enough to meet market rule, not merely Red Square/Black Sea/energy/humanitarian/future framework; (3) adverse implementation evidence and violations do not negate agreement reached unless they show the agreement was narrow or not mutual.

Source map: official Russian/Kremlin/Ushakov; Ukrainian presidential or Zelensky social confirmation; U.S. announcement; credible media consensus; adverse monitoring/implementation reports.

Native search/capture work: Web open captured Guardian May 8 article at https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/may/08/trump-russia-ukraine-three-day-ceasefire-9-may, lines 144-174, reporting both sides confirmed May 9-11 ceasefire, suspension of all kinetic activity, prisoner swap, Zelensky Telegram confirmation, and Ushakov Russian confirmation. Submitted and accepted as ev_236dce43c3d8_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-236dce43c3d8.

Captured Ukrainska Pravda English article at https://www.pravda.com.ua/eng/news/2026/05/08/8033888/, lines 67-90, reporting Zelenskyy said 1000-for-1000 POW swap would take place, confirmed Trump truce with Russia on 9-11 May, and quoted Zelenskyy saying Ukraine received Russia's agreement and that a ceasefire regime must be established on May 9, 10, 11. Accepted as ev_2c8cd49d9b00_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-2c8cd49d9b00.

Captured RFE/RL article at https://www.rferl.org/a/ukraine-russia-victory-day-cease-fire-drone-attacks/33752482.html, lines 95-183 plus adverse context 183-199, reporting Trump announced three-day cease-fire in war between Russia and Ukraine, both sides quickly confirmed, suspension of kinetic activity, 1000-prisoner swap, Zelensky confirmation, and Ushakov statement; also reports earlier/collateral violations and strikes. Accepted as ev_5d2cebff6f4b_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-5d2cebff6f4b.

Failed/unsubmitted capture paths: Direct X pages for Zelenskyy and WhiteHouse rendered zero text lines in web open; PBS article URL returned 403; Ukrinform URL opened but yielded no text lines; ISW May 8 and May 11 direct URLs returned 403 through web open. Treat these as search/capture limitations, not affirmative evidence. Earlier curl to Kremlin official page timed out after 12s. Case packet already contains official-source summary for Kremlin page.

Analysis: Plaintiff can now offer case-packet market rule (ev_f841be6ff80d), official-source summary (ev_959b165782b3), Guardian (ev_236...), Ukrainska Pravda (ev_2c8...), RFE/RL (ev_5d...). The combined evidence proves public mutual confirmation and broad ceasefire language. Defense context is real but best characterized as motive/occasion and compliance rather than scope limitation. A prisoner exchange can be part of a general ceasefire; temporary does not mean non-general.

Planned offered evidence labels: PX-1 market rule; PX-2 official-source record; PX-3 Guardian report; PX-4 Ukrainian-side Zelensky confirmation report; PX-5 RFE/RL Trump/both-side confirmation. Include technical report summarizing source-chain and capture gaps.

Defendant merits argument notes, opportunity arguments:defendant.

Plan: inspect new record and evidence list; read plaintiff's new evidence; investigate missing implementation/scope evidence; submit defense source captures before relying on them; update /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md; file merits argument with exhibits and concise search/capture ledger.

Record scan: get_case/list_evidence show plaintiff submitted three new evidence items: ev_236dce43c3d8 Guardian May 8, ev_2c8cd49d9b00 Ukrainska Pravda May 8, ev_5d2cebff6f4b RFE/RL May 8. Plaintiff offered them with market page and official-source packet, and filed a source-chain/capture ledger. No defense submitted evidence before this turn.

Evidence read: read_evidence_range for all three new plaintiff items. They strengthen plaintiff on public reports of a May 9-11 truce, Zelenskyy confirmation, Ushakov/Russian confirmation, and Trump's phrase suspension of all kinetic activity. They also preserve helpful defense context: prisoner swap, Red Square/parade focus, violation accusations, and continued strikes.

Native investigation searches: "May 9 2026 Russia Ukraine ceasefire violations May 9 10 11 ISW assessment"; "May 9 2026 Ukraine Russia reported violations three day ceasefire prisoner exchange"; "site:understandingwar.org May 9 2026 Russia Ukraine ceasefire Victory Day prisoner exchange kinetic activity". Search results identified ISW May 9/10/11 assessments as the strongest implementation source. Direct ISW pages were available as leads/snippets; accessible secondary captures included NV and CBS/AP.

Source pages opened:
- NV: https://english.nv.ua/russian-war/isw-says-fighting-continued-despite-may-9-11-ceasefire-50606640.html, lines 14-53. Reports ISW found offensive operations continued across the frontline on first day, intensity decreased but did not stop; Ukraine General Staff reported 51 engagements; Russian strikes continued in Kharkiv/Kherson; Russia claimed 8,970 violations; ISW said ceasefire lacked clearly defined conditions and only publicly known detail was Red Square non-targeting; Russia used ceasefire for rotations/reinforcements/logistics.
- CBS/AP: https://www.cbsnews.com/news/russia-ukraine-accuse-each-other-ceasefire-violations/, lines 96-118. Reports mutual accusations on May 10; Zelenskyy said Russia was not observing truce and there was no calm in front line areas despite lull in large-scale attacks; casualties from drone/artillery attacks; Russia claimed 1,000+ Ukrainian violations and response in kind; timeline somewhat unclear; previous ceasefires failed amid mistrust.
- Guardian May 9 Red Square article opened; not submitted because NV and CBS/AP are stronger for merits implementation/scope and time is limited.

Defense evidence submitted and accepted:
- ev_52a3277a9302_submitted-evidence-04-defendant-52a3277a9302, NV/ISW implementation and scope evidence.
- ev_d5ef4e09892f_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-d5ef4e09892f, CBS/AP mutual violation/no calm evidence.

Reasoning: Do not deny plaintiff has improved formation evidence. Argue plaintiff proves a three-day announced truce but not a qualifying general pause. The market excludes humanitarian pauses and limited agreements. Russian official-source packet and plaintiff's own exhibits frame the deal as prisoner exchange/Victory Day/Red Square. Implementation evidence matters as circumstantial proof of the agreement's character and scope, not as a requirement of perfect compliance. If forces continued offensive operations across the theater, conditions were undefined, and the only clearly known condition was Red Square non-targeting, the preponderance does not show a general pause in conflict.

Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Plaintiff rebuttal notes.

Record scan: get_case/list_evidence show defendant merits argument and two new defendant submitted evidence items: ev_52a3277a9302_submitted-evidence-04-defendant-52a3277a9302 (NV/ISW) and ev_d5ef4e09892f_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-d5ef4e09892f (CBS/AP). Used stat_evidence/read_evidence_range to read both exact contents.

DX-4/NV content: says Russian and Ukrainian forces continued offensive operations across the frontline during first day of May 9-11 ceasefire; intensity decreased; Ukraine reported 51 combat engagements; Russian forces continued strikes in Kharkiv and Kherson; Russia claimed 8,970 Ukrainian violations. Also says combat activity declined but did not stop completely; ISW noted ceasefire lacked clear conditions and only publicly known detail was Red Square non-targeting; ceasefire lacked mechanisms/monitoring/dispute resolution/troop withdrawals and was unlikely sustainable; Russia used period for rotations/reinforcements. Importantly for plaintiff, it also notes Trump announced May 9-11 ceasefire May 8 and Zelenskyy later confirmed agreement and planned 1,000-for-1,000 prisoner exchange.

DX-5/CBS/AP content: says Russia and Ukraine swapped accusations of breaking U.S.-brokered ceasefire; Trump had announced Russia and Ukraine would swap 1,000 prisoners as part of three-day ceasefire coinciding with Victory Day; Zelenskyy said Russia not observing and no calm in frontline areas despite lull in large-scale attacks; Russia accused Kyiv of 1,000+ violations and said Russia responded in kind; timeline somewhat unclear, but Trump said fighting suspended Saturday-Monday and both sides appeared to agree.

Analysis: Defendant evidence targets compliance, durability, monitoring, and operational follow-through. It does not negate formation of a public mutual agreement. DX-4 and DX-5 contain concessions: Trump announced, Zelenskyy confirmed, both sides appeared to agree; activity declined/lull in large attacks. Defense overreads “only publicly known detail” and Red Square context; plaintiff exhibits separately preserve broad language: ceasefire in the war between Russia and Ukraine, suspension of all kinetic activity, both sides confirmed, ceasefire regime May 9-11. Market rule focuses on official reaching of agreement by deadline, not perfect performance. No new source evidence needed for rebuttal; use visible plaintiff and defendant evidence already admitted. Offer DX-4/DX-5 as rebuttal exhibits if helpful because they are visible evidence IDs and support the compliance-vs-formation distinction.

Defendant surrebuttal notes, opportunity surrebuttals:defendant.

Record scan: get_case and list_evidence checked. Plaintiff rebuttal filed no new source evidence; it used defendant's DX-4/DX-5 as PX-R4/PX-R5 and submitted a rebuttal evidence audit. Evidence list unchanged except exhibit use.

Rebuttal point to answer: plaintiff says DX-4/DX-5 prove only weak compliance/monitoring and violation accusations; they concede announcement/agreement and do not show the arrangement was Red Square-only or not a public mutual ceasefire.

Analysis: This is plaintiff's strongest point, so surrebuttal should concede the legal premise that isolated violations do not erase a valid agreement. But defendant's evidence is not offered merely as breach evidence. It shows the alleged general halt lacked defined conditions, had only one clearly identified operational condition (Red Square non-targeting), continued offensive operations across the theater from day one, mutual violation accusations, and response-in-kind behavior. Those facts bear on the character and scope of the arrangement, which is part of plaintiff's burden because the market excludes humanitarian/limited arrangements and requires a general pause.

No additional outside search needed because rebuttal added no new factual source or quotation requiring verification; answer rests on existing record evidence and legal/evidentiary framing.

Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Plaintiff closing notes.

Final record scan: get_case/list_evidence after defendant surrebuttal. No new evidence after plaintiff rebuttal. Current record includes plaintiff evidence PX-3 Guardian, PX-4 Ukrainska Pravda, PX-5 RFE/RL; defendant evidence DX-4 NV/ISW and DX-5 CBS/AP; original case-packet files including market rule and official-source summary; defendant surrebuttal argues DX-4/DX-5 are scope/character evidence, not only compliance evidence. Closing phase does not allow new evidence or reports.

Closing analysis: Plaintiff has strongest proof on agreement reached and public mutual confirmation. PX-3 says both sides confirmed May 9-11 ceasefire and suspension of all kinetic activity. PX-4 says Zelenskyy confirmed Trump truce with Russia on 9-11 May and Russia's agreement, and that a ceasefire regime must be established May 9-11. PX-5 says Trump announced a three-day cease-fire in the war between Russia and Ukraine, suspension of all kinetic activity, and both sides quickly confirmed; Ushakov/Russia agreed. PX-2 supports Russian official-source path. PX-1 rule focuses on official agreement/wide credible reporting by deadline and excludes limited agreements/humanitarian pauses.

Defense strongest point: DX-4/DX-5 show continued operations, unclear mechanisms, mutual violations, Red Square as only clearly known operational detail, and poor implementation. This raises serious compliance/scope concern. Response: those exhibits also concede Trump announcement, Zelensky confirmation, and both sides appeared/agreed; activity declined/lull in large attacks suggests some ceasefire effect. Weak mechanisms and violations do not negate agreement reached. Red Square was additional concrete operational condition, not the whole agreement, because separate record language says ceasefire in the war/suspension of all kinetic activity/ceasefire regime May 9-11.

Decision: file closing concise, candid on gaps, emphasize preponderance. Do not cite private notes or unadmitted external sources.

Defendant closing notes, opportunity closings:defendant.

Record scan: get_case/list_evidence checked at the start of closing. Plaintiff closing filed, no new evidence submitted after surrebuttal. Closing phase does not allow new evidence. Existing decisive evidence remains market rule, official-source packet, plaintiff media captures, and defendant NV/CBS implementation captures.

Plaintiff closing theory: record proves public mutual dated agreement; violations are performance only; credible media consensus and "suspension of all kinetic activity" satisfy the proposition; prisoner exchange/Victory Day explain occasion but do not limit scope.

Defense closing theory: concede the record supports announcement of a short May 9-11 truce. The burden is stricter: plaintiff must prove it constituted a general pause in the conflict. The record is at least equally consistent with a U.S.-brokered, Victory Day/prisoner-exchange deconfliction with Red Square/parade-security condition and weak/undefined mechanisms. Market rule excludes humanitarian and limited arrangements. Russian official source frames acceptance as for prisoner exchange. Ukrainian official-adjacent decree is Red Square-specific and direct decree remains missing. Plaintiff's own PX-4/PX-5 center prisoner exchange and Red Square. DX-4/DX-5 show continued operations, no calm, unclear timeline/conditions, and mutual response-in-kind, which are evidence of scope/character, not just breach.

No further outside investigation needed or allowed for record admission in closing; close from admitted evidence. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.
